element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player assumes control of sole railway company with no competitors
Primary revenue source; requires route planning and passenger demand assessment
Alternative transport type enabling city growth; less profitable than passengers but unlocks construction
Auto-generated in response to material delivery; provides passenger demand
Auto-generated as city develops; enhances revenue and growth
Optional strategic buildings requiring proper area population and conditions for profitability
Emergent goal alongside financial success; managing growth through material distribution

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Open-ended, self-paced decision-making with optional competitive pressure from emergent city growth; no time limits or immediate crises

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Player-constructed buildings (ski resorts, hotels) represent optional strategic placement in suitable populated areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Steep learning curve; basic railway company management with passenger and materials transport introduction
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Financial and strategic complexity increases; player must balance multiple revenue streams and city development
difficulty_curve.late_game: Strategic building placement and optimization; maximizing profit through conditions-based special buildings
notable_spikes: Initial game complexity noted as 'steeply graded learning curve' by Computer Gaming World reviewer

notable_level_patterns.description: Game includes multiple pre-built scenarios (notably 'New Town' scenario) providing different starting conditions and city layouts
Open-ended design with no rival companies; player controls sole railway monopoly allowing emergent objectives
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any standard game after scenario selection

notable_level_patterns.description: A.III. Map Construction editor allows modification of saved games or creation of landscapes from scratch; 6 sample maps provided

sources_used: wikipedia - A-Train article (A-Train III/SNES-era release information)
